title: Statusline Component
description: Schema for a component.json manifest. A component is a directory (dir name == id) the loader places into a profile slot. type=segment runs IN-PROCESS (host sources render.sh and calls render__<id>, zero fork); type=line is FORKED as a standalone runtime process and may print multiple whole lines. Host + Claude both read this manifest.

$schema.description: Manifest format tag. Distinct from JSON-Schema $schema; identifies the component manifest version the host understands.

id.description: Stable slug, unique in the registry. Equals the component dir name and, for segments, the render function suffix render__<id>.

version.description: Semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, optional pre-release / build). Bump on every code change (CI-enforced).

description.description: One-line summary for Claude to scan the catalog.

type.description: segment = inline fragment, in-process bash, zero fork, returns one string via REPLY. line = standalone script, forked, prints one or more whole lines.

runtime.description: Interpreter for the render/fetch entry. Segments must be bash (the host sources them). Lines may use any interpreter.

render.description: How to produce the component's output every tick.

entry.description: Entry file relative to the component dir. Segment: render.sh defining render__<id>(). Line: a script the runtime executes.

ttl.description: Seconds the host may reuse cached line output before re-forking. 1 = fork every tick (smoothest). >1 = reuse cached output for N seconds. Lines only; segments are always in-process every tick.

fetch.description: Optional background data refresh for line components. Runs detached on its own ttl and atomically writes its own cache; render only reads that cache and never blocks the tick. null = no background fetch.

entry.description: Entry file relative to the component dir. Often the same file as render.entry, switched by an arg in args.

args.description: Extra args appended after the standard invocation to select fetch mode (e.g. ["--fetch"]).

ttl.description: Re-fetch once the cache is older than this many seconds.

self_managed.description: true = the component spawns its own detached --fetch from inside render (current widgets do this). false/absent = host schedules the fetch.

inputs.description: Dotted stdin-JSON paths the component depends on (e.g. context_window.used_percentage, session_id). Declares dependencies for visibility; the host projects these into CC_* env (see CONTRACT.md).

requires.description: Host prerequisites. If unmet the host skips the component.

bin.description: Executables that must be on PATH.

network.description: Hosts the component connects to. Non-empty implies network access is needed.

macos.description: macOS-only capabilities required (e.g. AppleEvents). Non-empty implies macOS-only.

config.description: Per-instance config surface. schema declares the knobs a profile may set under components[].config; defaults apply when a profile omits a key.

schema.description: Map of config key -> knob spec.

file.description: Default config file packaged in the component dir (e.g. symbols.default), used for list-valued config the loader cannot pass as flags.

placement.description: Default slot/order used when a profile lists the component without overriding them.

capabilities.description: Declared effects, for review and least-privilege auditing.

network.description: Hosts the component is allowed to reach. Empty = no network.

fs_write.description: Filesystem locations the component may write (e.g. $STATE_DIR for its fetch cache). Empty = no writes.

stdin_fields.description: Dotted stdin-JSON paths the component actually consumes (the projection allow-list).

then.description: Segments run in-process and cannot fork or fetch.

slot.description: Where the component renders. top/middle/bottom = line widgets (arbitrary lines). row1/row2 = segments joined with ' · '. rule = the host-drawn divider position.

order.description: Sort key within a slot; lower renders first.

configKnob.description: One user-tunable config key: its type (or enum), default, and a human description.

enum.description: Allowed values (in place of, or alongside, type).

default.description: Default value when the profile omits this key.
